states.description: Wired end-to-end and usable on this surface.
Not yet wired on this surface; a gap we intend to close.
Deliberately excluded from this surface by a linked platform decision.

capabilities.name: Block until a session matches a condition
capabilities.notes: A scripting/automation gate; the GUIs surface live state instead.

capabilities.name: List available repositories for new sessions
Type into another session remotely
capabilities.notes: An attached GUI types directly; the standalone remote-type command is a CLI convenience.

capabilities.name: View pending tool approvals
capabilities.notes: Native approval queues are removed in preparation for eliminating interactive approvals entirely under [issue #1392](https://github.com/d0ugal/graith/issues/1392).

capabilities.name: Respond to a tool approval
capabilities.notes: Native approval responses are removed in preparation for eliminating interactive approvals entirely under [issue #1392](https://github.com/d0ugal/graith/issues/1392).

capabilities.name: List / approve / revoke paired devices
capabilities.notes: Device-list administration is local-human-only and remote-denied. Native apps pair with a daemon but deliberately do not manage its trust list.

capabilities.name: Send direct messages
capabilities.notes: Native apps compose a direct message to a session's inbox from the session context menu.

capabilities.name: Read direct-message conversations
capabilities.notes: Native apps show a session's direct-message conversation with mark-as-read.

capabilities.name: Publish / subscribe to messaging topics
capabilities.notes: Topics are an agent and orchestrator coordination primitive rather than a native human-chat surface.

capabilities.name: Inspect / release jailed PR comments
capabilities.notes: Quarantined PR comments are an untrusted-input moderation workflow kept in the CLI.

capabilities.name: Start / stop / resume / inspect scenarios
capabilities.notes: Scenarios are operated through the CLI and orchestrator. Native apps show scenario-created sessions as ordinary sessions without scenario grouping or lifecycle controls.

capabilities.name: Completion actions and lifecycle cleanup
capabilities.notes: Scenario TOML and the CLI expose completion actions and cleanup state; native GUI presentation is deliberately excluded.

capabilities.name: Declare and publish scenario results
capabilities.notes: The CLI declares result contracts and reports durable publication state; native GUI publication and result-detail presentation are deliberately excluded.

capabilities.name: Scenario timeout, retry & quorum policy
capabilities.notes: Scenario runtime policies are CLI/daemon-only; iOS and macOS do not model or surface policy state.

capabilities.name: Manage todo items
capabilities.notes: Add, claim, assign, transition, and remove are agent/orchestrator operations; native apps deliberately do not edit todo state.

capabilities.name: View todo lists and progress
capabilities.notes: A read-only native overview is in scope; todo mutation remains CLI/orchestrator-only.

capabilities.name: List / status / run / pause / resume triggers
capabilities.notes: Trigger lifecycle is an automation control plane kept in the CLI and orchestrator.

capabilities.name: List / restart / inspect MCP servers
capabilities.notes: MCP server inspection and restart are developer operations kept in the CLI.

capabilities.name: Put / append / remove documents
capabilities.notes: Document mutation is an agent and scripting primitive; native apps retain the separate read-only browser.

capabilities.name: Browse and read documents (list keys, view a document body)
Send a desktop / push notification to the human
capabilities.notes: Agents and scripts send notifications; native apps are notification recipients and presenters.

capabilities.name: Explain / watch sandbox policy and denials
capabilities.notes: Sandbox explain/watch output is diagnostic and streaming, making the terminal its natural interface.

capabilities.name: Health check / doctor diagnostics (orphan GC stays CLI-only)
